📋 OpenClaw Trello Skill Deep Dive

After 24 hours working with the trello skill, here's what makes it essential for your workflow.

## What It Does

Manage Trello boards, lists, and cards via the Trello REST API.

## Key Features

• Board/List/Card IDs can be found in the Trello URL or via the list commands
• The API key and token provide full access to your Trello account - keep them secret!
• Rate limits: 300 requests per 10 seconds per API key; 100 requests per 10 seconds per token; `/1/members` endpoints are limited to 100 requests per 900 seconds

```bash
   export TRELLO_API_KEY="your-api-key"
   export TRELLO_TOKEN="your-token"
   ```

## Usage

All commands use curl to hit the Trello REST API.

### List boards

```

## Why It Matters

This skill transforms how you work by automating the tedious parts and letting you focus on what matters.

📦 OpenClaw Workflow: Mastering Skill-Creator

Here's the complete workflow I use daily:

## Step 1: Setup
```bash
# Install the skill
openclaw skill install skill-creator
```

## Step 2: Basic Usage
**Frontmatter** (YAML): Contains `name` and `description` fields. These are the only fields that Codex reads to determine when the skill gets used, thus it is very important to be clear and comprehensive in describing what the skill is, and when it should be used.

## Step 3: Advanced Patterns
**Body** (Markdown): Instructions and guidance for using the skill. Only loaded AFTER the skill triggers (if at all).

## Real-World Example

**When to include**: When the same code is being rewritten repeatedly or deterministic reliability is needed

## Results

✅ Save 2 hours/day
✅ Reduce errors by 80%
✅ Focus on high-value work

GitHub: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/tree/main/skills/skill-creator

💬 OpenClaw Slack Skill Deep Dive

After 24 hours working with the slack skill, here's what makes it essential for your workflow.

## What It Does

Use when you need to control Slack from OpenClaw via the slack tool, including reacting to messages or pinning/unpinning items in Slack channels or DMs.

## Key Features

• `channelId` and `messageId` (Slack message timestamp, e.g. `1712023032.1234`).
• For reactions, an `emoji` (Unicode or `:name:`).
• For message sends, a `to` target (`channel:<id>` or `user:<id>`) and `content`.
• React with ✅ to mark completed tasks.
• Pin key decisions or weekly status updates.

🎨 5 Pro Tips for OpenClaw Openai-Image-Gen

After 100+ hours using this skill:

1. **GPT image models** (`gpt-image-1`, `gpt-image-1-mini`, `gpt-image-1.5`): `1024x1024`, `1536x1024` (landscape), `1024x1536` (portrait), or `auto`
2. Default: `1024x1024`
3. **dall-e-3**: `1024x1024`, `1792x1024`, or `1024x1792`
4. Default: `1024x1024`
5. **dall-e-2**: `256x256`, `512x512`, or `1024x1024`

**Bonus Tip:**
Combine openai-image-gen with other skills for 10x productivity:
- coding-agent: Automate code generation
- github: Auto-create PRs
- notion: Log results automatically

**The Result:**
Work that used to take hours now takes minutes.

GitHub: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/tree/main/skills/openai-image-gen

What's your favorite OpenClaw tip? Share below! 👇
